title: Innodata Seizes Major U.S. Defense Contract and Eyes Future Opportunities
text: Innodata was awarded a Missile Defense Agency Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense (SHIELD) indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity (IDIQ) contract, a major development in the company's federal market expansion. The SHIELD IDIQ award enables Innodata to seek development, prototyping, and operations work on Missile Defense Agency systems, positioning the company to capture an estimated $25 million in federal contract revenue during 2026. This award represents validation of Innodata's federal sector capabilities and opens doors for subsequent task order awards across the defense contracting landscape. Concurrent with the defense contract, Innodata announced selection by Palantir Technologies to provide high-quality training data and data engineering services. The Palantir engagement leverages Innodata's expertise in specialized annotation, multimodal data engineering, and generative AI workflow support. In Q1 2026, Innodata Federal achieved traction with several U.S. government research agencies and specialized AI vendors, with engagement feedback from Palantir-supported computer vision work generating strong customer response. The company initiated work with a major federal systems integrator and was selected as finalist for potentially significant additional awards. These federal market developments complement strong momentum in private Big Tech customer relationships, with the new $51 million Big Tech contract and 453% year-over-year growth from existing Big Tech customers demonstrating balanced growth across both government and commercial segments.
